name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper-chest height. Step under the bar, place it on your upper traps (high bar) or rear delts (low bar), and grip the bar wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar and step back. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out. Keeping your chest up and core braced, squat down by bending at the knees and hips simultaneously. Go as low as you can while maintaining a neutral spine—ideally thighs parallel or lower. Drive through your midfoot to stand up.
form_cues: Keep the bar path vertical over midfoot
Chest up, don’t let your upper back round
Knees track in line with the second toe
Brace your core as if about to be punched
safety_notes: Always use a squat rack with safety pins set at appropriate height. If you cannot maintain a neutral spine, reduce weight and depth. Do not bounce at the bottom; control the movement. Cease immediately if you feel sharp back or knee pain.
source: Adapted from ExRx.net and NSCA guidelines
